message:
test(types): add proptest property-based tests for ffs-types

20 property-based tests covering:
- BlockSize validation and byte/block conversion roundtrips
- Alignment (align_down/align_up) invariants and bracketing
- Block group and inode group math inverse properties
- CRC32C seed equivalence and batch/scalar consistency
- DeviceId UUID roundtrip, Ext4InodeNumber/BtrfsObjectId roundtrips
- ByteOffset/BlockNumber checked arithmetic
- Read/write LE roundtrips, ensure_slice bounds, trim_nul_padded

Closes bd-1wfa.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1va","title":"Docs: Reconcile ext4/btrfs scope + phased support statements","description":"Goal: make sure every doc says the same thing about what FrankenFS V1 supports and what is explicitly excluded/phased.

Must be consistent across: COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC, PLAN, README, FEATURE_PARITY.

Scope items to pin down:
- ext4 v1: supports 1K/2K/4K block sizes; requires extents+filetype; read-only mount first; journaling replay and writes later.
- btrfs: phased; single-device sys_chunk mapping and read-only discovery first; no multi-device/RAID/compression initially.

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-1wtx","title":"Implement Lab-based deterministic concurrency tests","description":"# Implement Lab-based deterministic concurrency tests

## GOAL
Create deterministic tests for all concurrency-sensitive code using asupersync Lab runtime.

## BACKGROUND
From AGENTS.md: "Deterministic testability. Concurrency-sensitive logic must be testable under asupersync lab runtime."

Lab provides:
- Seed-controlled scheduling
- Reproducible interleaving enumeration
- Deterministic failure injection

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Lab Test Infrastructure
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod lab_tests {
    use asupersync::lab::{Lab, LabConfig};
    
    fn run_lab_test<F, Fut>(name: &str, seeds: &[u64], f: F)
    where
        F: Fn(Lab) -> Fut,
        Fut: Future<Output = ()>,
    {
        for &seed in seeds {
            let config = LabConfig::new().seed(seed);
            let lab = Lab::new(config);
            lab.block_on(f(lab.clone()));
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. MVCC Lab Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn lab_fcw_conflict_detection() {
    run_lab_test("fcw_conflict", &[0, 42, 12345], |lab| async {
        let store = MvccStore::new();
        
        let t1 = store.begin();
        let t2 = store.begin();
        
        lab.spawn(async { t1.write(block, data).await });
        lab.spawn(async { t2.write(block, data).await });
        
        let results = lab.join_all().await;
        // Exactly one should fail with conflict
        assert\!(results.iter().filter(|r| r.is_err()).count() == 1);
    });
}
```

### 3. Cache Lab Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn lab_arc_concurrent_access() {
    run_lab_test("arc_concurrent", &[0, 1, 2], |lab| async {
        let cache = ArcCache::new(100);
        
        for i in 0..10 {
            lab.spawn(async move {
                for j in 0..100 {
                    cache.get_or_insert(block(j), fetch_block).await;
                }
            });
        }
        
        lab.join_all().await;
        cache.verify_invariants();
    });
}
```

### 4. Failure Injection
```rust
lab.inject_failure("disk_read", 0.01); // 1% failure rate
lab.inject_delay("commit", Duration::from_micros(100));
```

## TESTS
1. lab_fcw_conflict_detection (multiple seeds)
2. lab_ssi_write_skew (multiple seeds)
3. lab_gc_concurrent_access (multiple seeds)
4. lab_cache_eviction_race (multiple seeds)
5. lab_failure_injection (verify recovery)

## LOGGING
- Lab seed (info): for reproduction
- Schedule trace (trace): for debugging
- Invariant violation (error): full state dump

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All concurrency-sensitive code has Lab tests
2. Tests pass with 1000 different seeds
3. Failures reproducible with seed
4. No flaky tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:56:34.819682372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:30:50.981442532Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:30:50.981418958Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lab","mvcc","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wtx","depends_on_id":"bd-22w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.886850216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wtx","depends_on_id":"bd-kro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:10.255676501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"bd-1wtx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2lpe","title":"Implement zero-copy I/O paths where possible","description":"# Implement zero-copy I/O paths where possible

## GOAL
Minimize data copies on I/O paths, applying mechanical sympathy principles.

## BACKGROUND
FUSE fundamentally requires kernel/userspace copies. But we can minimize userspace copies:
1. Direct buffer handoff between cache and FUSE
2. Vectored I/O for multi-block operations
3. Page-aligned allocations for OS optimization

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. BlockBuf Refcounting
```rust
pub struct BlockBuf {
    data: Arc<AlignedVec>,  // Shared, aligned buffer
}

impl BlockBuf {
    pub fn as_slice(&self) -> &[u8];
    pub fn clone_ref(&self) -> BlockBuf;  // Arc::clone
    pub fn make_mut(&mut self) -> &mut [u8]; // COW if shared
}
```

### 2. Aligned Allocation
```rust
pub struct AlignedVec {
    ptr: NonNull<u8>,
    len: usize,
    cap: usize,
}

impl AlignedVec {
    pub fn new(size: usize, align: usize) -> Self;
    // Align to page size (4096) for direct I/O compatibility
}
```

### 3. Vectored I/O
```rust
pub trait VectoredBlockDevice: BlockDevice {
    fn read_vectored(
        &self,
        blocks: &[BlockNumber],
        bufs: &mut [BlockBuf],
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<()>;
    
    fn write_vectored(
        &self,
        blocks: &[BlockNumber],
        bufs: &[BlockBuf],
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<()>;
}
```

### 4. FUSE Buffer Handoff
- Use fuser reply buffer directly where possible
- Avoid intermediate Vec allocations
- Consider mmap for large reads (if FUSE supports)

## TESTS
1. Unit: BlockBuf refcounting semantics
2. Unit: Aligned allocation alignment
3. Integration: Vectored I/O correctness
4. Benchmark: Copy count measurement

## LOGGING
- Buffer allocation (trace): size, alignment
- Copy detected (trace): source, dest, size
- Vectored I/O (trace): block count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. No unnecessary copies in hot paths
2. Memory usage not increased
3. Performance improvement measurable
4. Correctness preserved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:26.339499830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T15:21:53.291811560Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T15:21:53.291782436Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","optimization","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:00.318748579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:29.832494790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"bd-2lpe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2oa","title":"ffs-error: Decide mount-validation error variants (UnsupportedFeature/UnsupportedBlockSize/etc)","description":"Context: mount validation can fail because of unsupported feature bits, block sizes, or incompatible on-disk geometry.

Question: do we represent these as:
- ParseError::InvalidField (and map to FfsError::Format), OR
- dedicated FfsError variants (UnsupportedFeature, UnsupportedBlockSize, InvalidGeometry), OR
- an intermediate MountValidationError used by ffs-core.

Deliverables:
- Chosen approach documented.
- Error mapping yields correct errno (EINVAL vs EOPNOTSUPP) for FUSE and CLI.

Acceptance:
- ext4 validate_v1() failures are surfaced with stable errors and good UX.
- Docs/spec do not list non-existent FfsError variants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:59.187355332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:56.423539784Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:56.423519876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}
 {"id":"bd-2oah","title":"EPIC: Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (Graveyard Entry 15.4)","description":"# EPIC: Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (Graveyard Entry 15.4)

## PURPOSE
Scale WAL (Write-Ahead Log) throughput for concurrent MVCC writers by using per-core WAL buffers with epoch-based ordering. Current single WAL is a serialization bottleneck for concurrent transactions.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 15.4:
- Silo (Tu et al., SOSP 2013): per-core log buffers, epoch-based commit ordering
- Aether (Johnson et al., VLDB 2010): decoupled log buffer management, early lock release
- Key insight: WAL serialization is unnecessary if epoch ordering provides equivalent guarantees

## DESIGN

### Per-Core WAL Buffers
- Each CPU core has a dedicated WAL buffer (thread-local or core-affinity)
- Transactions write to their core's buffer without contention
- No central lock on WAL append

### Epoch-Based Ordering
- Global epoch counter advanced periodically (e.g., every 10ms or every N commits)
- Each WAL entry stamped with current epoch
- Durability guarantee: all entries in epoch E are durable before epoch E+1 begins
- Recovery: replay all entries in epoch order (entries within same epoch are commutative)

### Group Commit
- All WAL buffers for epoch E flushed together (group commit)
- Single fsync for all cores' buffers at epoch boundary
- Reduces fsync overhead from O(transactions) to O(epochs)

### Recovery
- On crash: find last complete epoch across all per-core logs
- Replay all entries up to last complete epoch
- Discard entries from incomplete epoch (they never committed)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Per-core WAL buffers eliminate contention
- [ ] Epoch-based ordering provides equivalent durability guarantees
- [ ] Group commit reduces fsync frequency
- [ ] Recovery correctly handles incomplete epochs
- [ ] Benchmark: WAL throughput scales with core count

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E WAL test (bd-2oah.4) passes
3. Parallel WAL scales linearly to 4+ cores
4. Group commit reduces fsync count by >= 50pct
5. Crash recovery replays all committed transactions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:54.753437422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:46.641784818Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:46.641711741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","durability","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:40.817042970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-2oah","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section.  {"id":"bd-2oah.1","title":"Design per-core WAL buffer for concurrent MVCC writes","description":"# Design per-core WAL buffer for concurrent MVCC writes

## GOAL
Design and implement per-core WAL buffers that eliminate contention on the write-ahead log for concurrent MVCC transactions.

## DESIGN

### Buffer Structure
- Thread-local WAL buffer: Vec<WalEntry> per core
- Core affinity: use thread_local! or core_affinity crate
- Buffer capacity: configurable (default 4MB per core)
- When buffer full: flush to disk, reset

### WAL Entry Format
```rust
struct WalEntry {
    epoch: u64,
    txn_id: TxnId,
    entry_type: WalEntryType,
    payload: Vec<u8>,
    crc32c: u32,
}
```

### Concurrency
- No locks on per-core buffers (thread-local access)
- Only contention point: epoch counter increment (atomic CAS)
- Flush can happen concurrently across cores

### Integration
- Replace single WAL in ffs-mvcc with per-core buffer pool
- TxnManager routes writes to current core's buffer
- Epoch manager coordinates global epoch advancement

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Per-core buffers avoid contention (no mutex on hot path)
- [ ] Buffer management handles full/flush correctly
- [ ] Core affinity works on Linux (sched_setaffinity or equivalent)
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent writes to separate buffers
- [ ] Benchmark: throughput vs single WAL baseline","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:05.486513103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:33:55.958516141Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:33:55.958434879Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:05.486513103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":84,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section.  {"id":"bd-tbn","title":"Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration","description":"# Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration

## GOAL
Implement btrfs transaction boundaries integrated with FrankenFS MVCC, bridging native btrfs semantics with our version-chain model.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs transactions are fundamentally different from ext4/JBD2:
- Transactions are tree-level, not journal-level
- Commit creates new tree roots
- Old roots preserved for snapshots
- This naturally maps to MVCC!

The key insight: btrfs transaction = MVCC transaction boundary.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Transaction Handle
```rust
pub struct BtrfsTransaction {
    /// MVCC transaction ID
    txn_id: TxnId,
    /// Snapshot for reads
    snapshot: Snapshot,
    /// Pending tree modifications
    pending_trees: BTreeMap<TreeId, TreeRoot>,
    /// Delayed refs accumulated
    delayed_refs: DelayedRefQueue,
    /// New blocks allocated
    allocated: Vec<BlockNumber>,
    /// Blocks to free on commit
    to_free: Vec<BlockNumber>,
}
```

### 2. Begin/Commit/Abort
```rust
impl BtrfsTransaction {
    pub fn begin(store: &MvccStore, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self>;
    
    pub fn commit(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<CommitSeq> {
        // 1. Flush delayed refs
        // 2. Update super block with new roots
        // 3. MVCC commit (FCW check)
        // 4. Persist to device
    }
    
    pub fn abort(self) {
        // Free allocated blocks
        // Discard pending changes
    }
}
```

### 3. Tree Root Updates
On commit:
- New tree roots written
- Super block updated with generation and roots
- Checksum tree updated
- Free space cache updated

### 4. Compatibility Mode
For mounting existing btrfs images:
- Read existing transaction log
- Initialize MVCC from latest committed generation
- Subsequent writes use MVCC

## TESTS
1. Unit: Begin/abort discards changes
2. Unit: Begin/commit persists changes
3. Integration: Concurrent transactions with disjoint trees
4. Integration: FCW conflict detection for same tree
5. Recovery: Crash before commit -> rollback

## LOGGING
- Transaction begin (debug): txn_id, generation
- Transaction commit (info): duration, trees modified
- Transaction abort (warn): reason

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ACID semantics preserved
2. Concurrent writers do not corrupt
3. Crash recovery to last committed state
4. MVCC snapshots see consistent trees","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:55.750691013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103301376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103251292Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transaction model over MVCC in ffs-btrfs with begin/commit/abort semantics, delayed-ref flush, tree-root staging, FCW conflict coverage, and workspace gates passing. Remaining core on-disk wiring is tracked as broader write-path follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-1b7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.509360853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:21.021338413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.626558426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'btrfs transaction parity' (currently ❌). Maps to transaction.c. When complete: flip to ✅ in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:50Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BTRFS TRANSACTION MODEL: btrfs transactions group multiple COW tree updates into an atomic unit. Key concepts: (1) transaction handle (acquire on begin, release on end), (2) delayed items (buffered in memory until transaction commit), (3) transaction commit writes all dirty trees atomically. Our MVCC layer maps naturally onto this: MVCC transaction ≈ btrfs transaction.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:59Z"},{"id":110,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Progress update (2026-02-13): implemented  in  with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing. Added new unit tests covering abort-discard semantics, commit persistence, concurrent disjoint-tree commits, FCW conflict on same tree, and drop-without-commit rollback behavior. Updated  row for btrfs transaction parity to 🟡 with remaining gap note. Gates run and passing: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:32:27Z"},{"id":111,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Correction: implemented BtrfsTransaction in crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs with MVCC-backed begin/commit/abort, staged tree-root metadata records, delayed-ref flush before commit, allocation/free bookkeeping, and structured tracing.
